licensed_object: Benchmark-scoped or evaluation-scoped claims about tasks, protocols, model versions, metrics, model-as-judge limits, evaluation datasets, or measured evaluation surfaces.
G_binding: evaluated task/scenario set; prompt or evaluation protocol; model/version; metric; judge/evaluator; sample and time scope
Q_binding: report only the evaluated surface or a safely weaker version
U_binding: interpretation of evaluation evidence for claim audit; not deployment, paper acceptance, or human replacement authority
allowed_actions: ACCEPT; REWRITE; WEAKEN; SUPPORT_ONLY; SUPPRESS; REJECT_OR_OUT_OF_SCOPE; NEEDS_TEMPLATE_ADMISSION
forbidden_stronger_claims: Benchmark score proves broad general intelligence; model-as-judge agreement proves human replacement; evaluation result proves deployment safety; benchmark coverage proves all-task competence; evaluation tool use decides paper acceptance.
boundary_note: Keep claims benchmark/task/protocol scoped unless the visible evidence directly licenses a stronger statement.

licensed_object: Claims about what a resource, software package, metadata record, schema, release, annotation workflow, identifier, or documentation artifact records or makes available.
G_binding: documented object; schema/specification; metadata field; release or identifier scope; provenance/access context; workflow/resource boundary
Q_binding: report documentation and metadata support only; do not transfer to correctness, legal clearance, security, deployment readiness, scientific validity, or complete reproducibility
U_binding: resource review and claim-audit context; not compliance certification
allowed_actions: ACCEPT; REWRITE; WEAKEN; SUPPORT_ONLY; SUPPRESS; REJECT_OR_OUT_OF_SCOPE; NEEDS_TEMPLATE_ADMISSION
forbidden_stronger_claims: Metadata proves the underlying result is correct; release packaging proves security or deployment readiness; identifier/provenance proves all claimed results; documentation standard proves legal clearance; workflow description proves scientific conclusion correctness.
boundary_note: Documentation can support inspectability and reuse claims, but not downstream truth, security, legality, or complete reproducibility by itself.

licensed_object: Claims about calibration, probabilistic prediction, uncertainty quantification, diagnostics, calibration metrics, uncertainty libraries, probabilistic programming, or conformal/calibration support.
G_binding: metric/tool/library/procedure; model family if visible; evaluation or diagnostic scope; calibration/uncertainty object; stated data or future-use assumptions
Q_binding: report only the same calibration/uncertainty diagnostic or a safely weaker version
U_binding: empirical or methodological claim audit; not deployment safety, scientific truth, proof correctness, or universal uncertainty coverage
typed_preorder: background_context < tool_or_metric_description < calibration_or_uncertainty_diagnostic < bounded_uncertainty_support < future_perfect_calibration_or_complete_uq < deployment_safety_or_scientific_truth
allowed_actions: ACCEPT; REWRITE; WEAKEN; SUPPORT_ONLY; SUPPRESS; REJECT_OR_OUT_OF_SCOPE; NEEDS_TEMPLATE_ADMISSION
forbidden_stronger_claims: Calibration metric proves the model will never be wrong; calibration tool proves all future data are calibrated; uncertainty library solves every UQ task; probabilistic programming proves causal/scientific truth; diagnostic plots certify deployment safety.
boundary_note: Calibration and uncertainty evidence is useful, but background/support-only boundaries remain the main observed failure point.
